brand_focus: Cooking tips, recipes, and marinades with AI-styled ingredient photography. Audience is Europe-first, US a strong second. Goal: grow followers fast-but-safe by making every post highly shareable (sent to a friend) and saveable.

slots.note: Thu ~12:23 — previously used slot, retained for consistency. Sauce kept as primary theme; no data differentiates themes so holding best-branded option. Minute jittered.
Tue ~18:44 — previously used slot, retained. Marinade kept; brand-core theme with strong recipe utility, likely to attract saves once non-follower reach improves. Minute jittered.

Sat ~11:08 — weekend slot retained. Technique has 3 posts with zero engagement but so does every theme; no statistical basis to retire it yet. Hold and monitor one more week before dropping. Minute jittered.
Mon ~19:31 — second sauce slot retained. All slots return identical reach (2.0) so no slot is worse than another; distributing across Mon+Thu gives weekday spread. Minute jittered.

content_plan.sauce: A versatile sauce, glaze, or dressing built around a single memorable ratio. Slide 1: hero shot of finished sauce — glossy, richly coloured, on a clean minimal surface, absolutely no text, numbers, or labels anywhere on the image. Slide 2: sauce shown in context — spooned over protein or pooling on a wooden board, purely photographic. Slides 3-4: the same sauce applied to two visually distinct foods (e.g. charred chicken AND roasted cauliflower) to signal versatility and prompt DM sends of 'which would you use this on?'. Final slide: extreme close-up of sauce texture. ALL ratios, quantities, ingredient lists, and usage ideas live in the caption only — never on the image.
content_plan.marinade: One complete marinade built around a single memorable ratio. Slide 1: bold hero shot of marinated protein at its most visually striking — deeply coloured, glistening, no text on image. Slide 2: clean flat-lay of raw ingredients arranged aesthetically, nothing labelled or annotated. Slides 3-4: sequential process shots — protein submerging into marinade, then the vivid colour transformation after marinating — purely visual contrast, zero overlaid text. Final slide: finished plated dish in beautiful light. ALL ratios, marinating times, substitution tips, save CTAs, and send prompts belong in the caption only.
content_plan.technique: One practical, slightly surprising kitchen technique that produces a visible, dramatic result. Slide 1: the 'after' outcome at its most striking — peak visual payoff, no text. Slide 2: tight close-up of the key physical action (knife angle, fold, crust formation) — purely photographic. Slides 3-4: visual contrast showing the difference the technique makes using only the difference in the food itself, no labels or overlaid text. Final slide: clean beauty shot of the finished result. ALL explanatory language, the rule being demonstrated, surprising fact, and send prompts go in the caption only.

caption_strategy: Every caption must front-load a search-friendly hook in the first line (e.g. 'The marinade ratio that works on anything'). Follow with the full practical payload — ratios, quantities, steps, substitutions — since this is the information people save and share. End with a send prompt framed as a question or gift ('Send this to someone who always asks you for your sauce recipe'). Use natural cooking-keyword language throughout (marinate, glaze, umami, caramelise) so the caption surfaces in Instagram search. Keep tone warm and knowledgeable, not listicle-generic. Always include an explicit save CTA ('Save this before you meal-prep'). Total caption length: 150-300 words.
hashtag_strategy: Use exactly 4-5 hashtags, placed at the end of the caption or in the first comment. Prioritise mid-size cooking-specific tags (300k-3M posts) over giant generic ones where the account will be invisible. Rotate across: one ingredient-specific tag (e.g. #MisoMarinade), one technique/method tag (e.g. #HomemadeSauce), one meal-context tag (e.g. #WeeknightDinner), and one broad discovery tag (e.g. #CookingTips). Avoid tags above 10M posts entirely at this account size. Refresh the tag set weekly based on which posts attract any non-follower reach above the current 2.0 baseline.
learnings: All 12 posts return identical reach (2.0) and zero sends, saves, or follows across every theme and slot — this is almost certainly organic floor reach (likely followers/close connections only), not algorithm distribution. No theme or slot is statistically better. Priority action: captions must be aggressively search-optimised and save-baiting since that is the only lever available before the account earns algorithmic reach. Hold cadence at 4/week — do not increase. Do not retire any theme yet; with uniform zero-engagement data, theme differences are noise. Reassess after next 8 posts.
